# Decisions docs

This index is generated from markdown files in `docs/decisions`.

| Document                                                                                                                                   | Description                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [ADR-0003: Afenda tenancy as the only business workspace authority](./ADR-0003-afenda-tenancy-authority.md)                                | Tenants and Afenda memberships are canonical ERP workspace and audit jurisdiction; not neon_auth.organization or unmapped Better Auth org IDs.           |
| [ADR-0004: Identity authority — Better Auth user vs Afenda principal (identity_links)](./ADR-0004-identity-bridge-and-principals.md)       | Mandatory identity_links bridge from Better Auth user to Afenda users; bootstrap on sign-up; no email fallback for runtime resolution.                   |
| [ADR-0005: Auth schema authority — public (self-hosted Better Auth) vs neon_auth](./ADR-0005-auth-schema-authority-public-vs-neon-auth.md) | public Better Auth tables are the live runtime auth store; neon_auth has no runtime authority — no reads, no writes, no workspace semantics in app code. |
| [ADR-0006: Session operating context (Better Auth) + audit resolution rules](./ADR-0006-session-operating-context.md)                      | session.additionalFields + setSessionOperatingContext; identity_links only; session is validated lens not authority.                                     |
| [ADR template](./ADR_TEMPLATE.md)                                                                                                          | Reusable template for documenting architecture decisions with triggers, ownership, and review cadence.                                                   |
| [ADR-0001: Core web architecture baseline](./ADR-0001-core-web-architecture-baseline.md)                                                   | This ADR is intentionally conservative during growth. It does not block tactical improvements in API boundary, testing depth, or developer tooling.      |
| [ADR-0002: Trigger-based architecture upgrades](./ADR-0002-trigger-based-upgrades.md)                                                      | Consider migration only when recurring route/search-param defects or refactor friction are measured over consecutive iterations.                         |
